# feat(runtime): add lifecycle hooks for Worker + WorkflowScheduler (#914)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `97d8d303740e` — feat(runtime): add lifecycle hooks for Worker + WorkflowScheduler (#914)

**Body**:

Workflow operators have to wrap every body in try/except today to surface
task / scheduler failures to ops. This adds first-class registration
hooks so a single line — `worker.on_task_failure(my_alerter)` — routes
typed events to Slack, Sentry, custom HTTP, etc.

- `Worker.on_task_{prerun,postrun,success,retry,failure}` accept sync OR
  async handlers and receive a frozen `TaskEvent` (task_id, workflow_name,
  attempt, max_attempts, worker_id, elapsed_ms, exception). Handler
  exceptions are caught + logged at WARN per observability.md Rule 3a so
  a faulty alerter cannot break the task lifecycle.
- `WorkflowScheduler.on_job_{success,error,missed}` chain a SECOND
  APScheduler listener alongside the existing private bookkeeping
  listener; handlers receive a frozen `JobEvent`.
- `kailash.observability.alerts.{slack,webhook}` reference adapters
  consume the typed payloads — copy-extend for Discord/Sentry/PagerDuty.

Tier 2 wiring tests under `tests/integration/runtime/` exercise the
contracts end-to-end: real APScheduler ticks for the success path, real
Redis + Worker for the task path (skips when Redis is offline), and
synthetic-event dispatch for paths LocalRuntime swallows internally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
